title: Kefteji
subtitle: Tunisian fried vegetables with egg
excerpt: Courgette, potato, and pepper deep-fried separately until coloured and tender, then combined and roughly crushed together with fried egg, tomato, garlic, and cumin. The mixing creates a rough, textured salad where the crisp vegetables and broken egg meld together. Served at room temperature.
sourceNotes: Kefteji is a Tunisian street food typically served with offal (liver and merguez) at roadside stalls. The vegetable-only version is the vegetarian preparation. The name derives from the Turkish kofte (meatballs), though the dish as made in Tunisia often involves no meat. The fried vegetables are crushed roughly, the texture is deliberately rough, not smooth.

content.text: The vegetables are fried individually in the same oil so each cooks at the right pace. The potato goes in first as it takes longest; the courgette and pepper follow. Keep the oil at medium-high heat, too cool and the vegetables absorb oil rather than frying cleanly.

The crushing step at the end is not mashing. Use a fork to roughly break the vegetables into irregular pieces, leaving plenty of distinct chunks. The egg is fried separately and stirred through broken up, its yolk becomes the binding sauce that holds the dish together.

Heat the {{vegetable-oil}} in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Add the {{potato}} cubes and fry for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden and cooked through. Add the {{courgette}} and {{pepper-red}} and fry for 5 to 6 minutes until coloured and soft. Remove all the vegetables with a slotted spoon and set aside. In the same pan, fry the {{eggs}} in the remaining oil, one or two at a time, until the whites are set. Set aside. Discard most of the oil, leaving 1 tablespoon. Add the {{garlic}}, {{cumin-ground}}, and {{paprika-sweet}} and cook for 30 seconds. Add the {{tomato}} and cook for 3 minutes until softened. Return the fried vegetables to the pan. Roughly crush everything with a fork, mixing in the fried eggs and breaking the yolks. Stir in the {{lemon-juice}} and {{coriander}}. Add {{sea-salt-fine}} to taste. Serve warm or at room temperature.

items.fix: The oil should be hot enough that the vegetables sizzle on contact. Too cool and they absorb oil instead of frying. Drain on kitchen paper and blot before returning to the pan
items.cause: Oil temperature too low during frying

items.fix: Use ripe but not overly juicy tomatoes. Cook the tomatoes longer to reduce their liquid before mixing in the vegetables
items.cause: Tomatoes too juicy or added without reducing

items.fix: Cut to 1.5 cm maximum. Fry covered for the first 5 minutes to steam the potato through, then uncover to colour
items.cause: Diced too large or heat too low

content.text: Kefteji is Tunisian street food, sold from small restaurants and market stalls that specialize in it. The classic version is served with fried liver, merguez sausage, and a scoop of harissa alongside the vegetable mixture. The all-vegetable version is the home preparation and increasingly popular as a standalone dish. It is a useful side dish for grilled meats or fish and works well as part of a spread of Tunisian small dishes.
